title: Missing Bit Flips in Binary Representation

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.outputFormat: Count the total number of bit flips (1 to 0 or 0 to 1) in the binary representation of the number.
statement.notes: The task is to count the total number of bit flips, not the total number of ones or zeros in the binary representation.

examples.explanation: Binary representation of 5 is 101. There are 2 bit flips: 1 to 0 in the first position and 0 to 1 in the third position.

editorial.title: Missing Bit Flips in Binary Representation
editorial.optimalSolution: To count the number of bit flips, we can perform a bitwise XOR operation between the input number and the number with all bits set to 1 (i.e., the maximum possible value for the input). This will result in a number where all bits to the left of the first 1 are 1 and all bits to the right of the first 0 are 0. The number of 1s in this resulting number is the total number of bit flips.
